editor_note: The body prose is genuinely well-edited native Arabic technical writing: domain terminology is correct and fully consistent throughout — بوابة (gate), كيوبتات (qubits), دائرة (circuit), مستورد/مُصدِّر (importer/exporter), دالة/وسيط/معامل cleanly distinguishing function/argument/parameter, plus idiomatic renderings like "تراعي الربع" (quadrant-aware) and "حرفية المواصفة" (letter-of-the-spec). The informal register (يمكنك، استخدم، يجب عليك) holds consistently with no formal antum slips, and I found no semantic drift in any translated sentence. Two non-misleading defects keep this from a clean PASS: (1) line 52 contains a stray, fully-untranslated English bullet "- Review the [Verify your program](/guides/debugging-tools) topic." injected between the API-reference sentence and the "Import simple programs" heading — this content has no counterpart in the English source at that location (it belongs only to the Next steps block, which is separately and correctly translated at AR line 333), so it is both contamination/drift and raw English left in an Arabic file; (2) line 56 mistranslates "you can simply use ... with a single argument" by dropping the adverb "simply" as a dangling adjective "بسيط" that agrees with nothing ("استخدام qasm2.load وqasm2.loads بسيط"), reading as broken Arabic where "ببساطة" or a restructure was needed. Neither error misleads a reader about the actual API behavior, hence MINOR_ISSUES rather than FAIL.

examples.source: See the OpenQASM 2 Qiskit API ... for more information.\n\n### Import simple programs
examples.translation: راجع [واجهة برمجة تطبيقات OpenQASM 2 في Qiskit](...) لمزيد من المعلومات.\n- Review the [Verify your program](/guides/debugging-tools) topic.
examples.suggested: Delete the stray bullet — the EN has nothing between this sentence and the '### Import simple programs' heading; this 'Verify your program' link belongs only to the Next steps block (already translated as 'اطلع على موضوع [التحقق من برنامجك]').
examples.why: A whole untranslated English line is injected here that does not exist in the source at this position; it is both content drift and raw English left untranslated in an Arabic file.

examples.source: you can simply use qasm2.load and qasm2.loads with a single argument.
examples.translation: يمكنك استخدام `qasm2.load` و`qasm2.loads` بسيط مع وسيط واحد فقط.
examples.suggested: يمكنك ببساطة استخدام `qasm2.load` و`qasm2.loads` مع وسيط واحد فقط.
examples.why: The adverb 'simply' is rendered as a dangling adjective 'بسيط' that agrees with no noun and reads as broken Arabic; the adverb ببساطة (or a restructure) is required.

editor_note: This is overwhelmingly native, well-edited technical Arabic that teaches as clearly as the English; meaning is faithful throughout (I checked the trace-out/partial-trace derivations, the trace-preservation and complete-positivity conditions, the "at least four Kraus matrices" claim, and the maximally-entangled Choi-state caveat — no semantic drift). Core terminology is consistent and conventional: قناة, كيوبت, وحدوية (unitary), مصفوفة الكثافة, الأثر الجزئي (partial trace), التشابك/المتشابكة (entanglement), شبه موجب (positive semidefinite), الإيجابية الكاملة (complete positivity), and the names ستاينسبرينغ/كراوس/تشوي are transliterated consistently. Three slips keep this from a clean PASS: (1) "Stinespring form" at line 78 is rendered بالصيغة الاسبرينغية — a malformed adjective that drops "Stine-" and clashes with the clean ستاينسبرينغ used everywhere else; a native writer would say بصيغة ستاينسبرينغ. (2) "completely depolarizing" is rendered إزالة القطبية rather than the more standard physics term إزالة الاستقطاب — understandable and used consistently, but slightly non-standard. (3) "isometry" rendered تشاكلية (line 88) is an unusual coinage versus the conventional تقابل متساوي القياس; defensible and not misleading. None of these mislead the reader.

examples.source: we say that it is expressed in *Stinespring form* or that it's a *Stinespring representation*
examples.translation: نقول إنها معبَّر عنها *بالصيغة الاسبرينغية* أو أنها *تمثيل ستاينسبرينغ*
examples.suggested: نقول إنها معبَّر عنها *بصيغة ستاينسبرينغ* أو أنها *تمثيل ستاينسبرينغ*
examples.why: الاسبرينغية is a malformed adjective that drops the 'Stine-' of the name and reads as a coined non-word; it also clashes with the clean transliteration ستاينسبرينغ used consistently elsewhere in the file.

examples.source: completely depolarizing channel
examples.translation: قناة إزالة القطبية الكاملة
examples.suggested: قناة إزالة الاستقطاب الكاملة
examples.why: إزالة الاستقطاب is the more standard Arabic physics rendering of 'depolarizing'; القطبية (polarity) is understandable and used consistently here, but is the slightly non-standard choice.

examples.source: for an *isometry* $A$
examples.translation: لـ *تشاكلية* $A$
examples.suggested: لـ *تقابل متساوي القياس* $A$ (أو تماثل قياسي)
examples.why: تشاكلية is an unusual coinage for 'isometry'; the conventional term invokes distance/length preservation. Defensible and not misleading, but a domain reader may not immediately recognize it.

editor_note: Much of the prose is genuinely fluent, native-grade technical Arabic (the Introduction, Theory, and geometric-picture sections read as if written by an Arabic-speaking quantum engineer: تراكب for superposition, تضخيم السعة for amplitude amplification, قلب الطور for phase flip are all correct and consistent). But it fails on two grounds. (1) The core term "Grover operator" is rendered THREE inconsistent ways across the file: مشغّل غروفر (lines 109, 250, 387, 819), مؤثر غروفر (lines 248, 267, 792, 826), and معامل غروفر in the entire Q&A section (lines 903, 914, 918, 935) — and معامل literally means "coefficient/factor", a domain-wrong choice, while the conventional quantum-operator term is مؤثر. (2) Genuine semantic/terminology drift: line 721 translates "Minesweeper has been proven to be NP-complete" as «ثبت أن Minesweeper مكتملة من الناحية NP-hard» — it both swaps NP-complete for the distinct class NP-hard and produces the self-contradictory phrase "complete from the NP-hard side", which misteaches a complexity-theory fact. Separately, register breaks down: large stretches (lines 582, 650, 664, 670–672, 700, 713) drop into colloquial/dialectal Arabic ("تقدر تلاقيه", "تقدر تعرضها", "من المفترض تحصل", "إنك"/"إنه" for أنك/أنه) while the rest is MSA, and line 546 contains a broken half-transliterated token «البت-ستring» mixing Arabic and Latin letters. The terminology inconsistency plus the NP-complete mistranslation together warrant FAIL.

examples.source: the "Grover operator"
examples.translation: مشغّل غروفر … / مؤثر غروفر … / معامل غروفر …
examples.suggested: مؤثر غروفر (use consistently throughout)
examples.why: A single core term is rendered three ways in one file; معامل means "coefficient", which is domain-wrong for a quantum operator. The conventional rendering مؤثر should be used everywhere for consistency and correctness.

examples.source: Minesweeper has been proven to be NP-complete: it is hard to solve but easy to check.
examples.translation: ثبت أن Minesweeper مكتملة من الناحية NP-hard: يصعب حلها لكن يسهل التحقق منها.
examples.suggested: ثبت أن Minesweeper مكتملة على NP (NP-complete): يصعب حلّها لكن يسهل التحقق منها.
examples.why: NP-complete is silently changed to the distinct class NP-hard, and «مكتملة من الناحية NP-hard» is self-contradictory — a misstatement of a complexity-theory fact in teaching material.

examples.source: Make sure you save the file somewhere you can easily find it. … you could visualize it
examples.translation: تأكد إنك تحفظ الملف في مكان تقدر تلاقيه بسهولة … تقدر تعرضها بصرياً
examples.suggested: تأكد من حفظ الملف في مكان يسهل عليك إيجاده … يمكنك عرضها بصريًا
examples.why: These passages drop into colloquial/dialectal Arabic (تقدر، تلاقيه، إنك for أنك) while the surrounding document is MSA, producing an inconsistent voice mid-file.

examples.source: increase the size of the target bitstring to be arbitrarily long
examples.translation: زيادة طول البت-ستring المستهدف إلى ما لا نهاية
examples.suggested: زيادة طول سلسلة البت المستهدفة إلى ما لا نهاية
examples.why: «البت-ستring» is a broken token mixing Arabic and Latin letters; earlier the same concept was correctly rendered سلسلة البت (line 312), so this is both a typo and an inconsistency.

examples.source: Grover's algorithm can be run with fewer than t iterations and still amplify the target states.
examples.translation: يمكن تشغيل خوارزمية غروفر بعدد أقل من $t$ تكرارات وما زالت تضخيم الحالات المستهدفة.
examples.suggested: … ومع ذلك تُضخّم الحالات المستهدفة.
examples.why: «وما زالت تضخيم» is ungrammatical — a verbal noun (تضخيم) where a finite verb (تُضخّم) is required; reads as raw MT in this clause.

editor_note: This reads largely like a Czech-speaking quantum engineer wrote it: imperative second-person ("Připrav tříqubitový stav GHZ", "transpiluj obvod", "Seskup hradla a měření") flows naturally, the informal "ty" register holds throughout (including the allowed "Chceš-li"), and there are no formal Vy/Vám slips. Domain terminology is correct and consistent: qubit/hradlo/obvod/backend/shots are rendered conventionally, "twirling" and "samplex" are sensibly kept in English with Czech declension, "expectation values" → "středních hodnot" is the right statistical convention, and the file even correctly distinguishes "expectation values" (střední hodnoty) from the scalar "expected value: 1.0" (očekávaná hodnota) mirroring the English wording shift on line 422. Meaning is faithful end-to-end with no inverted conditions or dropped caveats — the uniqueness definition (lines 298–302) and the 900-randomizations/1000-shots explanation (line 351) are precise. Two minor naturalness blemishes keep this from a clean PASS: "Post-procesuj"/"post-procesuj" (lines 197, 422) is an anglicism a native technical writer would more likely render as "Zpracuj"/"Dodatečně zpracuj", and the one prose rendering of "pass manager" as "správce přechodů" (line 262) is a literal calque where the field usually leaves the term untranslated as it is elsewhere in the file. Neither misleads the reader.

examples.source: Post-process and analyze the results.
examples.translation: Post-procesuj a analyzuj výsledky.
examples.suggested: Zpracuj (dodatečně) a analyzuj výsledky.
examples.why: "Post-procesuj" is an anglicism/neologism; a native technical writer would use "zpracovat"/"dodatečně zpracovat". Appears twice (lines 197 and 422).

examples.source: Use the pass manager to transpile the circuit to become an ISA circuit.
examples.translation: Použij správce přechodů k transpilaci obvodu na ISA obvod.
examples.suggested: Použij pass manager k transpilaci obvodu na ISA obvod.
examples.why: "správce přechodů" is a literal calque of pass+manager; the field conventionally keeps "pass manager" untranslated, and the rest of the file leaves it as the code identifier. Minor inconsistency, not misleading.

editor_note: This reads as genuine, well-edited Czech technical prose, not machine translation — phrasings like "Začněme tím, že se zamyslíme nad kvantovým stavovým vektorem", "necháváme θ nabývat hodnot", "o něco později nám to věci zjednoduší" and the discourse markers "Jinými slovy" / "Intuitivněji řečeno" are exactly what a Czech-speaking physicist would write, with no calqued English syntax. Core terminology is correct and consistent throughout: "matice hustoty" (density matrix), "stavový vektor", "čisté/smíšené stavy", "Pauliho matice", "globální fáze", and the EN sphere/ball distinction is faithfully preserved as "Blochova sféra" vs. "Blochova koule"; coordinate vocabulary ("sférické/kartézské souřadnice", "polární/azimutální úhel", "protilehlým bodům" for antipodal) is all conventional. I verified the meaning-bearing claims — the β=0⇒θ=0 / α=0⇒θ=π conditions, the "na jih od severního pólu" and "na východ od nultého poledníku" Earth analogy, and the center-of-sphere averaging — and found no semantic drift or dropped caveats; "až na globální fázi" correctly renders "up to a global phase." The only blemish is orthographic: "qubit" is consistently capitalized as "Qubit/Qubitů/Qubity" (lines 10–16 onward), whereas it is a common noun and should be lowercase; this is cosmetic and does not mislead. The exposition is entirely first-person plural ("Začněme", "máme"), so no formal/informal "ty"/"Vy" slip arises.

examples.source: qubit states
examples.translation: stavy Qubitů
examples.suggested: stavy qubitů
examples.why: "qubit" is a common noun and is conventionally lowercase in Czech (as in English). The translation capitalizes it as "Qubit/Qubitů/Qubity" consistently throughout the file (lines 10, 11, 13, 15, 16, ...). Consistent but orthographically incorrect; cosmetic only, no impact on meaning or terminology choice (the English loanword "qubit" itself is the right rendering).

editor_note: Overall this is fluent, well-edited Czech technical prose that mostly reads as if a Czech-speaking physicist wrote it, with correct and consistent core terminology: "moment hybnosti" (angular momentum), "magnetický moment", "hradlo"/"Hadamardovo hradlo" (gate), "obvod" (circuit), "stavový vektor", "vlastní hodnoty/vektory/stav" (eigenvalues/vectors/state), "transpilovat", "backend" and "shoty" sensibly kept as conventional English-derived terms, and "pozorovatelné veličiny" for observables — all defensible field conventions, used consistently. The physics meaning is faithful throughout: negations, quantities and caveats (20 % pravděpodobnější, ~1 %, 50:50, ±ℏ/2, the "operator action ≠ measurement" emphasis) are all preserved, and the informal "ty" register with inclusive mohl/a forms holds. The issues are isolated surface slips, not meaning errors: (1) line 60 "číselný poměr relacionovaný s typem" uses the non-word "relacionovaný" where a native writer would say "související s"; (2) line 365 "kvantový analogický pendant experimentů" is redundant translationese (analogický + the German loan "pendant"); (3) line 365 "získali jedno krajní hodnoty" has broken case agreement (should be "jednu krajní hodnotu"); (4) line 672 "mohl/a by ses říct" has a stray reflexive "se" that doesn't belong with "říct"; (5) minor within-file inconsistency between "průmět" (lines 161/169) and "projekce" (used everywhere else) for projection — both valid but mixed. None mislead the learner, so the page teaches as clearly as the English.

examples.source: adding a numerical ratio related to the type of angular momentum
examples.translation: se přidává číselný poměr relacionovaný s typem momentu hybnosti
examples.suggested: se přidává číselný poměr související s typem momentu hybnosti
examples.why: "relacionovaný" is not a real Czech word — a botched Latinism a native technical writer would never use; "související s" is the natural rendering.

examples.source: this is the quantum computing analog of the experiments done with particles
examples.translation: toto je kvantový analogický pendant experimentů prováděných s částicemi
examples.suggested: toto je kvantová obdoba experimentů prováděných s částicemi
examples.why: "analogický pendant" is redundant translationese stacking the German loan "pendant" onto "analogický"; "kvantová obdoba" reads naturally.

examples.source: it might indeed have surprised us that we obtained one extremum on our first measurement
examples.translation: mohlo by nás skutečně překvapit, že jsme při prvním měření získali jedno krajní hodnoty
examples.suggested: ...že jsme při prvním měření získali jednu z krajních hodnot
examples.why: Broken case/number agreement: "jedno ... hodnoty" mixes neuter singular with feminine plural; should be "jednu krajní hodnotu" or "jednu z krajních hodnot".

examples.source: In one sense, you might say
examples.translation: V jistém smyslu by ses mohl/a říct
examples.suggested: V jistém smyslu bys mohl/a říct
examples.why: The reflexive "se" does not belong with "říct" in this construction; it reads as a grammatical slip. The informal "ty" register itself is correct and consistent.

examples.source: projection (of angular momentum / of spin)
examples.translation: průmět (lines 161, 169) vs projekce (lines 569, 648, 702, 822, ...)
examples.suggested: Pick one — "projekce" dominates, so use it throughout
examples.why: Within-file inconsistency: the early sections use "průmět", the rest use "projekce". Both are valid Czech but a single file should stay consistent.

editor_note: This reads as competent, native-level German technical prose with faithful meaning throughout. Core domain terminology is correct and used consistently: "Rauschmodell" (noise model), "Quantenfehler" (quantum error), "verrauschte Simulation" (noisy simulation), and "Schaltkreis" (circuit), while "Qubit", "Gate", "Backend", "Transpiler-Pass" and "Primitives" are correctly kept in English per German Qiskit convention. The informal "du" register holds cleanly across the whole file (e.g. "du das ... verwendest" L33, "Du kannst" L71, "Beachte" L314/L450, "du solltest" L340) with no Sie-slips. No semantic drift: conditions, probabilities (0.05, p_reset etc.) and the constraint $T_2 \le 2T_1$ all match, and the pedagogical bullet lists (L390-393) explain the toy model as clearly as the English. The only blemishes are isolated naturalness/compound-clunkiness items that do not mislead: the triple-compound calque "Spielzeug-Rauschmodell-Beispiel" (L388) for "toy noise model example", the slightly stiff "in Anwesenheit von Fehlern" (L33), and the awkward hyphenated compound "Ein-Qubit-thermischer-Relaxationskanal" (L97). The hyphenated "Auslese-Fehler" (vs. the more standard closed "Auslesefehler") is a defensible, consistent choice. None of these rise above MINOR; verdict PASS.

examples.source: Let's consider a simple toy noise model example common in quantum information theory research:
examples.translation: Betrachten wir ein einfaches Spielzeug-Rauschmodell-Beispiel, das in der Quanteninformationstheorie-Forschung üblich ist:
examples.suggested: Betrachten wir ein einfaches Spielzeug-Rauschmodell, wie es in der Forschung zur Quanteninformationstheorie üblich ist:
examples.why: The triple compound 'Spielzeug-Rauschmodell-Beispiel' is a stacked calque of 'toy noise model example' that a native technical writer would unstack; dropping 'Beispiel' and recasting the genitive reads more naturally. Cosmetic, not misleading.

examples.source: for simulating quantum circuits in the presence of errors
examples.translation: zur Simulation von Quantenschaltkreisen in Anwesenheit von Fehlern
examples.suggested: zur Simulation von Quantenschaltkreisen bei Vorhandensein von Fehlern
examples.why: 'in Anwesenheit von' is a literal rendering of 'in the presence of' that is slightly stiff for inanimate 'Fehler'; 'bei Vorhandensein von' / 'unter dem Einfluss von' is idiomatic. Understandable as-is.

examples.source: a single qubit thermal relaxation channel parameterized by relaxation time constants
examples.translation: ein Ein-Qubit-thermischer-Relaxationskanal, parametrisiert durch Relaxationszeitkonstanten
examples.suggested: ein thermischer Ein-Qubit-Relaxationskanal, parametrisiert durch Relaxationszeitkonstanten
examples.why: Hyphenating the inflected adjective 'thermischer' into the compound ('thermischer-Relaxationskanal') is non-standard German orthography; pulling the adjective out front fixes it. Meaning fully preserved.

editor_note: This reads as native, well-edited German technical prose, not raw MT. The informal "du" register is held consistently throughout (führt dich, Lass uns, wirst du, Bemerke, wenn du tiefer eintauchen möchtest) with zero Sie/Ihnen slips. Domain terminology is correct and internally consistent: "Hamiltonian" is kept in English everywhere (incl. "Quanten-Hamiltonians"), "Quantum Circuit"/"Circuit" and "Gates" stay English by a defensible convention used consistently, and "Qubits", "Kohärenz-/Dephasierungszeiten", "Quantenzustandsraum", "Variationsalgorithmen", "Quantenvorteile" and "Fehlerkorrektur/Fehlerminimierung" are all rendered correctly and used the same way every time. No semantic drift, inverted negations, or swapped quantities were found — the "what quantum computing is not" list, the scaling/quality argument, and the speedup claims all match the English faithfully. The only blemishes are cosmetic naturalness slips: "Kondensierte Materie-Physik" (L57) is a calque where a native physicist would write "Physik der kondensierten Materie" or "Festkörperphysik", and "rechnerisch unlösbar" (L78) for "computationally intractable" leans slightly toward "unsolvable" rather than "intractable". Neither misleads the learner, so the verdict stays PASS.

examples.source: Condensed matter physics: studying strongly correlated systems
examples.translation: Kondensierte Materie-Physik: Untersuchung stark korrelierter Systeme
examples.suggested: Physik der kondensierten Materie: Untersuchung stark korrelierter Systeme
examples.why: "Kondensierte Materie-Physik" is a calque of the English compound; the conventional German field name is "Physik der kondensierten Materie" (or "Festkörperphysik"). Grammatical and understandable, but reads slightly translated.

examples.source: often become computationally intractable as the problem size grows
examples.translation: werden oft rechnerisch unlösbar, wenn die Problemgröße zunimmt
examples.suggested: werden oft rechnerisch nicht mehr handhabbar / praktisch unlösbar
examples.why: "intractable" means computationally hard, not strictly impossible; "unlösbar" slightly overstates toward "unsolvable." Minor in a pedagogical context, not misleading.

editor_note: This reads as genuine, well-edited German technical prose written by someone fluent in the field, not as machine translation. Domain terminology is correct, conventional, and internally consistent: "Schaltkreis" (circuit), "Gate" kept in English, "Superposition", "Interferenz", "Verschränkung" (entanglement), "Quantenparallelismus", "transpilieren", "Abfragemodell"/"Abfrage" (query model/query), "Black Box"/"Orakel", "balanciert"/"konstant", "Skalarprodukt"/"binäres Skalarprodukt" (dot product), and "Phase-Kickback" are all handled the way a German quantum-computing instructor would. The informal "du" register holds throughout (Probiere es, Schau dir an, Mach dir keine Sorgen, Dein tatsächlicher Verbrauch) with no Sie slips. Crucially, the load-bearing claims survive intact: the exponential-vs-deterministic / no-significant-speedup-vs-probabilistic distinction (line 543), "nur eine polynomielle Beschleunigung" (689/699), the worst-case $2^n/2+1$ query count, and the "respectively" mapping via "bzw." (290) all match the English, and the pedagogical accordion answers explain the interference/phase-kickback intuition as clearly as the source (the "interferieren destruktiv ... bleibt nur $|s\rangle$ übrig" expansion at line 599 even sharpens it). Only two trivial stylistic blemishes, neither misleading: the doubled "etwas etwas Überraschendes" at line 304 (pronoun + adverb collide, a native would write "etwas durchaus/leicht Überraschendes"), and the stacked "um ... um ..." purpose clauses at line 249 read slightly clunky though the meaning is fully preserved.

examples.source: note that something a bit surprising happens
examples.translation: beachte, dass etwas etwas Überraschendes passiert
examples.suggested: beachte, dass etwas durchaus Überraschendes passiert
examples.why: The pronoun 'etwas' (something) and adverb 'etwas' (a bit) collide into 'etwas etwas', which reads clumsy; a native technical writer would disambiguate with 'durchaus' or 'leicht'. Trivial, not misleading.

examples.source: we would need to query the box two times to get both f(0) and f(1)
examples.translation: müssten wir die Box zweimal abfragen, um zu wissen, ob die ... Funktion balanciert oder konstant ist, um sowohl f(0) als auch f(1) zu erhalten
examples.suggested: müssten wir die Box zweimal abfragen, um sowohl f(0) als auch f(1) zu erhalten und so zu wissen, ob die Funktion balanciert oder konstant ist
examples.why: Two back-to-back 'um ...' purpose clauses stack awkwardly; reordering reads more idiomatically. Meaning is fully preserved, so this is a polish nit only.

editor_note: This is a cloud-networking page (VPE/VPC connectivity to IBM Quantum Platform) with essentially no quantum-domain vocabulary, so the editorial focus is on infrastructure terminology and fluency, both of which are handled well. The Spanish reads as native technical prose — phrasings like "mediante una dirección IP que tú especifiques, asignada desde una subred dentro de tu VPC," "abarca todas las zonas de disponibilidad de tu VPC," and "sobre la red troncal privada" are idiomatic, not calqued. Cloud terminology follows a consistent, defensible IBM-Cloud convention: "endpoint" and "gateway" are correctly kept in English throughout (gateway de endpoint, gateway VPE), "private backbone" becomes "red troncal privada," and "CRN" is rendered as "Nombre de recurso en la nube," all used consistently. The informal "tú" register holds across every imperative (conéctate, especifiques, debes, completa los siguientes pasos, Consulta) with no usted/utilice/ejecute slips. No semantic drift: notably, line 18 "IBM Quantum Platform solo es accesible a través de la red pública" faithfully preserves the English source's own wording (line 17) rather than silently "correcting" the apparent contradiction with the surrounding private-connectivity context — the correct editorial choice. Meaning, caveats (e.g., "independientemente de si se accede a través de un endpoint público o VPE"), and pedagogical step ordering all match the English exactly.

This reads as genuine native Spanish technical prose, not machine output: sentences like "el circuito con $k$ pasos temporales tiene $4k$ capas de puertas de dos qubits" and "el resultado ideal de tales circuitos es trivialmente el estado de entrada... que tiene los valores esperados triviales para observables de Pauli arbitrarios" flow exactly as a Spanish-speaking quantum engineer would write them, with no calqued English syntax. Core domain terminology is correct and fully consistent: qubit, observable, "valor(es) esperado(s)" for expectation value, "puertas" for gates, "modelo de ruido" for noise model, "transpilar", "nivel de resiliencia"; brand/field terms that stay English (backend, shots, sampler, Estimator, ZNE, gate twirling, Trotterizados) are handled the conventional way. Informal "tú" register holds throughout (Descarga, Ten en cuenta, Modifica, Prueba, consulta, Puedes encontrar) with zero usted/usted slips, and meaning is faithful everywhere — no inverted conditions, dropped caveats, or softened claims. The only debatable item is "El Estimator soporta los siguientes niveles" where "soporta" is a mild Anglicism for "admite"/"es compatible con", but it is fully idiomatic in Spanish quantum/software docs and not a defect; "computadores cuánticos" (Latin-American convention) is also applied consistently. The pedagogy teaches as clearly as the English, including the toy-circuit and compute-uncompute explanations.

The core domain term "gate" is handled inconsistently and ungrammatically throughout the central "Quantum operators (gates)" section, which is disqualifying for teaching material. It appears three ways within the file: (a) left untranslated as English "Gate"/"Gates" ("dos tipos de Gates cuánticos", "Gate H Hadamard", "Gate CNOT", "cuantos más Gates"); (b) translated as "puerta" but with WRONG masculine agreement everywhere — "los puertas cuánticos", "estos puertas", "el puerta X", "el puerta Z", "Este puerta", "los puertas lógicos" — when "puerta" is feminine and requires "la puerta / las puertas"; and (c) capitalized mid-sentence as a pseudo-proper-noun. A native Spanish quantum engineer would never write "el puerta Hadamard." Secondary terminology inconsistencies: "Circuit" left in English in headings/steps ("Optimiza tu Circuit", line 588/310) while "circuito" is used elsewhere, and "Backend" capitalized as a proper noun (line 391). The non-gate prose is otherwise fluent and native (e.g. "ni siquiera la naturaleza lo ha determinado aún", "sigue cuestionando y poniendo a prueba las reglas científicas") and the informal "tú" register holds consistently, so the failure is squarely terminology/grammaticality on the most pedagogically load-bearing term, not register or fluency. One minor pronoun-agreement slip ("sin perturbarlo" should be "perturbarla", line 242) and a title/heading split between "Teletransportación" and "Teleportación" compound the inconsistency.

examples.source: sometimes visualizing quantum mechanical gates using matrices and vectors can be helpful ... we present here the matrix/vector forms of quantum gates
examples.translation: a veces visualizar los puertas mecánico-cuánticos mediante matrices y vectores puede ser útil ... presentamos aquí también las formas matricial/vectorial de los puertas
examples.suggested: a veces visualizar las puertas cuánticas mediante matrices y vectores puede ser útil ... presentamos aquí también las formas matricial/vectorial de las puertas cuánticas
examples.why: 'puerta' is feminine; 'los puertas' / 'estos puertas' is grammatically wrong. A native technical writer always uses 'la puerta / las puertas (cuánticas)'. This mis-gendering recurs throughout the file.

examples.source: we will primarily use two types of quantum gates: the Hadamard gate, the CNOT gate. A few others will play a lesser role: the X gate, Z gate
examples.translation: utilizaremos principalmente dos tipos de Gates cuánticos: el Gate Hadamard y el Gate CNOT. Algunos otros tendrán un papel menor: el puerta X, el puerta Z
examples.suggested: utilizaremos principalmente dos tipos de puertas cuánticas: la puerta Hadamard y la puerta CNOT. Algunas otras tendrán un papel menor: la puerta X, la puerta Z
examples.why: Within one sentence 'gate' is both the untranslated English 'Gate' and the mis-gendered 'el puerta'. The conventional Spanish term 'puerta (cuántica)' should be used consistently with feminine agreement.

examples.source: H Hadamard Gate / CNOT Controlled-NOT Gate
examples.translation: Gate H Hadamard / Gate CNOT (NOT controlado): Este puerta utiliza dos qubits
examples.suggested: Puerta H (Hadamard) / Puerta CNOT (NOT controlado): Esta puerta utiliza dos qubits
examples.why: Capitalized English 'Gate' as a heading label plus 'Este puerta' (wrong gender) in the same block — the term is neither translated consistently nor agreed grammatically.

examples.source: Optimize your circuit for running on real quantum hardware / Step 2: Optimize your circuit
examples.translation: Optimiza tu Circuit para ejecutarlo en hardware cuántico real / Paso 2: Optimiza tu Circuit
examples.suggested: Optimiza tu circuito para ejecutarlo en hardware cuántico real / Paso 2: Optimiza tu circuito
examples.why: 'Circuit' is left in English in a step list and a heading while 'circuito' is used correctly everywhere else in the body — a within-file inconsistency on a core term.

examples.source: it is not trivial to move quantum information without disturbing it
examples.translation: no es trivial mover información cuántica sin perturbarlo
examples.suggested: no es trivial mover información cuántica sin perturbarla
examples.why: The clitic 'lo' refers to 'información' (feminine) and must be 'la'. Minor agreement error, not meaning-altering, but reinforces the lack of a native final pass.

editor_note: Overall this reads as competent, mostly native French technical prose: the informal "tu" register is held consistently (Commence, prépare, crée, Compare, Comprends, Parcours, Apprends), and core terminology is well chosen and consistent — "charge de travail" (workload), "atténuation d'erreurs" (error mitigation), "post-sélection," "shots" kept in English, "qubits spectateurs," and "bruit non markovien (corrélé)" are all correct conventional renderings. Two slips keep it from a clean PASS. First, line 238 "il est probable insuffisant de se fier uniquement..." is an ungrammatical calque of "it is likely insufficient"; a native writer would say "il est probablement insuffisant" or "il est vraisemblablement insuffisant" — meaning is recoverable but it reads as raw MT at that spot. Second, English terms kept untranslated are inconsistently capitalized: "Transpiler" is capitalized as a proper noun in "passes de Transpiler" while the verb "transpiler" is lowercase, and "Backend" is capitalized in line 39 ("un Backend") against the otherwise-standard lowercase convention; "gate" is kept in English throughout (defensible, though "porte" is the more standard French quantum term — at least applied consistently). A minor third flattening: line 195 renders "the measured ones" as "les mesures" (the measurements) rather than "les distributions mesurées," slightly losing the parallel with "la distribution... idéale." None of these mislead the reader; the teaching content and the closing caveat about sampling overhead and complementary techniques land with the same nuance as the English.

examples.source: it is likely insufficient to rely solely on postselection for more complex or deeper circuits
examples.translation: il est probable insuffisant de se fier uniquement à la post-sélection pour des circuits plus complexes ou plus profonds
examples.suggested: il est probablement insuffisant de se fier uniquement à la post-sélection pour des circuits plus complexes ou plus profonds
examples.why: "il est probable insuffisant" is an ungrammatical calque of "likely insufficient"; French needs an adverb (probablement/vraisemblablement) or "il est probable qu'il soit insuffisant". Reads as raw MT.

examples.source: transpile against a backend ... transpiler passes
examples.translation: transpiler par rapport à un Backend ... passes de Transpiler
examples.suggested: transpiler par rapport à un backend ... passes du transpileur (ou: passes de transpilation)
examples.why: Inconsistent capitalization of kept-English terms: verb "transpiler" lowercase but noun "Transpiler" capitalized, and "Backend" capitalized here while the term is conventionally lowercase in French Qiskit usage.

examples.source: compute the distance between the ideal probability distribution and the measured ones
examples.translation: calcule la distance entre la distribution de probabilité idéale et les mesures
examples.suggested: calcule la distance entre la distribution de probabilité idéale et les distributions mesurées
examples.why: "les mesures" (the measurements) loses the parallel with "distribution... idéale"; the English contrasts the ideal distribution with the measured distributions.

editor_note: With one exception this is excellent, native French technical prose: fluent and idiomatic ("laisse à désirer visuellement", "en repartant à chaque fois d'une copie fraîche de l'état", "lèvera une erreur", "moteur de rendu"), with correct and consistent domain terminology ("vecteur d'état", "mesure dans la base standard", "opération/matrice unitaire", "phase globale près", "loi des grands nombres", "norme euclidienne") and the informal "tu" held throughout (Si tu souhaites, Utilise, Remarque, toi-même) with no vous/veuillez slips. The single blocking defect is a semantic/pedagogical drift at line 83: the English says matrix multiplication "can be performed using the `@` operator", but the French renders this as "à l'aide de la fonction `matmul` de `NumPy`". This both changes the source meaning and breaks the explanation-to-code link — the code cell immediately below (M1 @ ket1, M1 @ M2, M @ M) demonstrates the `@` operator, never `matmul`, so a French reader is told to use a function name that never appears in the worked example. Everything else (sample-count figures 5/9, 4/9, 556, 444; the global-phase caveat; print/display nuance) is faithful.

examples.source: Matrix multiplication, including matrix-vector multiplication as a special case, can be performed using the `@` operator.
examples.translation: La multiplication matricielle, y compris le produit matrice-vecteur comme cas particulier, peut être effectuée à l'aide de la fonction `matmul` de `NumPy`.
examples.suggested: La multiplication matricielle, y compris le produit matrice-vecteur comme cas particulier, peut être effectuée à l'aide de l'opérateur `@`.
examples.why: Semantic drift plus pedagogical mismatch: the source teaches the `@` operator and the very next code cell uses `@` (M1 @ ket1, M @ M), never `matmul`. Naming `matmul` instead breaks the explanation-to-example correspondence and tells the reader to use something the example does not show.

editor_note: This reads like genuine native French physics-pedagogy prose, not machine output: idiomatic choices such as "trancher le désaccord entre Einstein et Born", "Faisons un pas en arrière", "les possibilités sont grandes ouvertes", and "(à peine)" for the English "(barely)" show a human editor's hand, and the informal "tu" register is held consistently (tu pars, ne t'inquiète pas, rappelle-toi, peux-tu imaginer) with no vous/veuillez slips. Core domain terminology is correct and internally consistent: "intrication"/"intriquées" (entanglement), "moment angulaire" (angular momentum), "valeur d'espérance" (expectation value, L193 — the right conventional term, not a calque), "états propres/valeur propre" (eigenstates/eigenvalue), "porte"/"qubit"/"circuit quantique", "variables cachées", and the field-standard practice of keeping "spin", "sampler", "shots", "counts", "backend", "StatevectorSampler" in English inside French scaffolding. Semantic fidelity is high throughout the dense probability and bra-ket derivations — the ≤/< distinction in the 4/9 proof, the "if N₁=N₈=0" conditions, the conservation-of-angular-momentum caveats, and the "~50%, nettement supérieure à 4/9" conclusion all match the source exactly; note that "$P_{same,gm}$" at L840 reproduces a typo already present in the English source (should be "qm"), so it is not a translation defect. The only real blemish is a cosmetic spelling inconsistency for "non-local": rendered "non locale" (L846) and "« physique non locale »" (L76) but "non-locale"/"non-local" elsewhere (L943) — purely orthographic and non-misleading.

examples.source: the possibilities are wide open
examples.translation: les possibilités sont grandes ouvertes
examples.why: Idiomatic native rendering of an informal English figure of speech; a literal calque ('les possibilités sont largement ouvertes') would read stiffer. Confirms human-quality fluency, not MT.

examples.source: one could ask about the expectation value given by
examples.translation: on pourrait interroger la valeur d'espérance donnée par
examples.why: 'valeur d'espérance' is the correct conventional French physics term for expectation value (not the literal 'valeur d'attente' or the over-translated 'valeur moyenne'); used consistently.

examples.source: non-local
examples.translation: non locale / non-locale
examples.why: Cosmetic orthographic inconsistency: appears unhyphenated as 'non locale' (L76, L846) and hyphenated as 'non-locale' (L943). Does not affect meaning or comprehension; flagged only for consistency.
examples.suggested: Standardize on one form, e.g. 'non locale' (unhyphenated, common in modern French physics writing).

editor_note: This reads as competent, native-sounding Hebrew technical prose, not raw MT. Core domain terminology is correct and consistent throughout: "ערכי ציפייה" (expectation values), "נאמנות" (fidelity, e.g. "נאמנות העצירה" for stopping fidelity), "קיוביט"/"קיוביטים" (qubit), "מעגל" (circuit), and field-standard transliterations/English-kept terms ("טרנספילציה" for transpile, "backend", "ansatz", "המילטוניאן"). Register is consistently informal-imperative ("כתוב", "התחל", "הרץ", "שים לב"), appropriate for teaching material, and the conceptual explanations (AQC target/ansatz, Trotter steps, MPS) teach as clearly as the English. The defects are isolated and non-misleading: (1) line 532 "actionable information" rendered as "מידע פעיל" (active information) rather than "מידע מעשי"/"ניתן לפעולה" — a weak/slightly-off word choice; (2) line 147 "on how to fix" calqued as "על כיצד לתקן", which a native writer would phrase "כיצד לתקן"; (3) line 22 gender mismatch "מוצגת ייצוג" (should be masculine "מוצג ייצוג") plus a mild drift of "is found" → "מוצגת"; (4) "observables" flattened to "אופרטורים" (operators) at lines 20/455 rather than the more precise "תצפיתים"/"גדלים נמדדים" — defensible and self-consistent, but it loses the specific quantum sense. None of these distort meaning, invert a claim, or break the pedagogy.

examples.source: These can provide actionable information about the progress of the Hamiltonian simulation workflow.
examples.translation: אלה יכולים לספק מידע פעיל לגבי התקדמות זרימת העבודה של סימולציית המילטוניאן.
examples.suggested: אלה יכולים לספק מידע מעשי / מידע ניתן לפעולה לגבי התקדמות זרימת העבודה...
examples.why: "מידע פעיל" means "active information," not "actionable." Softens the meaning; a native technical writer would use "מעשי" or "ניתן לפעולה."

examples.source: returning an informative error message for how to fix the error
examples.translation: החזרת הודעת שגיאה אינפורמטיבית על כיצד לתקן את השגיאה
examples.suggested: ...הודעת שגיאה אינפורמטיבית כיצד לתקן את השגיאה / לגבי אופן תיקון השגיאה
examples.why: "על כיצד" is a calque of English "on how to"; idiomatic Hebrew drops "על" before "כיצד".

examples.source: a set of observables to estimate expectation values
examples.translation: קבוצת אופרטורים להערכת ערכי ציפייה
examples.suggested: קבוצת תצפיתים (observables) להערכת ערכי ציפייה
examples.why: "observables" flattened to the generic "אופרטורים" (operators); loses the specific quantum sense, though it is used consistently and is technically defensible (observables are Hermitian operators).

examples.source: a matrix product state representation of this circuit is found
examples.translation: מוצגת ייצוג מצב מכפלת מטריצות (MPS) של המעגל הזה
examples.suggested: מחושב ייצוג מצב מכפלת מטריצות (MPS) של המעגל הזה
examples.why: Gender mismatch: "ייצוג" is masculine so verb should be "מוצג"/"מחושב", not feminine "מוצגת"; also "found/computed" drifts to "מוצגת" (is displayed).

editor_note: This reads as genuine, fluent Hebrew technical prose written by someone who understands the material, not raw MT — sentences like "ויסייע לנו היטב מבחינה פדגוגית" and the explanatory flow in the parity/syndrome sections preserve the English intuition and informal teaching register well. Domain terminology is sound and consistent: סיבית for the classical bit, תסמונת (with a (syndrome) gloss) for syndrome, זוגיות (parity), משפט אי-השכפול for the no-cloning theorem, and Gate/Circuit/Hadamard correctly kept in English. Meaning is faithful throughout — probability formulas, the syndrome/correction tables, and all worked examples match the source with no inverted conditions or softened claims. The only blemishes are cosmetic and do not mislead: (1) "qubit" is cased inconsistently across the file, sometimes Qubit (e.g. "ב-Qubit", "ה-Qubit האמצעי" lines 15, 92, 102) and sometimes lowercase qubit (lines 75, 113, 143, 159) — sometimes mixed within one sentence; (2) a gender-agreement slip on line 33, "איזו משתי הערכים" where ערכים is masculine and should take "איזה משני"; (3) a typo "למעיל" for "למעלה" (above) on line 211 plus the slightly archaic "מלעיל" on line 175. These are isolated polish items, not mistranslations.

examples.source: qubit (lowercase, used consistently in English)
examples.translation: ב-Qubit / מצב qubit / ה-Qubit האמצעי / איזה qubit
examples.suggested: Pick one casing (lowercase qubit) and apply uniformly
examples.why: The same term alternates between capitalized Qubit and lowercase qubit throughout, sometimes within a single sentence — a casing inconsistency a native technical editor would normalize.

examples.source: determining which of the two binary values appears twice
examples.translation: קביעה איזו משתי הערכים הבינאריים מופיעה פעמיים
examples.suggested: איזה משני הערכים הבינאריים מופיע פעמיים
examples.why: Gender-agreement slip: ערכים is masculine, so the feminine איזו / משתי / מופיעה do not agree. Grammatical polish, meaning unaffected.

examples.source: an analogous table to the one from above
examples.translation: טבלה מקבילה לזו שלמעיל
examples.suggested: טבלה מקבילה לזו שלמעלה
examples.why: למעיל is a typo for למעלה (above); also מלעיל on line 175 is an unusual archaic form for the same idea. Minor surface errors, not misleading.

editor_note: This short, dense reference guide reads as native, well-edited Indonesian technical prose, not machine translation. Core domain terminology is correct and internally consistent: "qubit", "basis komputasional" (computational basis), "keadaan" (state), "eigenstate" and "idle/non-idle" sensibly kept in English, and "Circuit" deliberately kept untranslated and capitalized everywhere (headings and body, e.g. "Menerapkan pengukuran ke Circuit", "Pengukuran di tengah Circuit") — a defensible, consistent convention. Semantics are faithful throughout: the measure_active sentence "ukuran yang sama dengan jumlah qubit non-idle yang diukur" exactly mirrors "a size equal to the number of non-idle qubits being measured", and "tumpang tindih" for the Pauli-Z overlap is an understandable, accurate choice. Register holds informal-neutral with "Anda"/"Kami" and no deferential bapak/ibu. The only blemish is a trivial cosmetic capitalization slip in the intro paragraph — "basis Pauli-$Z$ satu-Qubit" capitalizes Qubit mid-phrase, inconsistent with the lowercase "qubit" used everywhere else — which does not affect meaning or fluency.

examples.source: that is, the single-qubit Pauli-$Z$ basis
examples.translation: yaitu basis Pauli-$Z$ satu-Qubit
examples.suggested: yaitu basis Pauli-$Z$ satu-qubit
examples.why: Cosmetic only: 'Qubit' is capitalized mid-phrase, inconsistent with the lowercase 'qubit' used consistently throughout the rest of the file. No meaning impact; a copy-edit nit, not a translation defect.

editor_note: This is strong, native-feeling technical Indonesian that teaches as clearly as the English. Domain terminology is correct and consistent: "fermion tak-terbedakan" (indistinguishable fermions), "operator kreasi dan anihilasi", "operator Pauli/bosonik", "antisimetri di bawah pertukaran", and "pemetaan" for the mapping process, while sensibly keeping field-standard English for tool/jargon terms ("mapper", "complete active space", "self-consistent field", "low-rank", "software", "workflow"). The informal "kamu"/"-mu" register holds throughout (molekulmu, ruang aktifmu) with no deferential slips. Meaning is faithful in the load-bearing spots — the energy-subtraction relation at line 130 and the qubit-0-rightmost ordering caveat at line 520 are both preserved. Two isolated polish slips keep it from a clean PASS: line 42 opens "Untuk menghasilkan Hamiltonian... melibatkan langkah-langkah" where the stray "Untuk" leaves the subject dangling (a native writer would drop it or recast as "Menghasilkan... melibatkan..."), and line 94 shifts to plural "banyak dari kalian" amid an otherwise singular-"kamu" file. Neither misleads.

examples.source: Generating a quantum chemistry Hamiltonian for use on IBM Quantum QPUs involves the following steps:
examples.translation: Untuk menghasilkan Hamiltonian kimia kuantum yang siap digunakan pada QPU IBM Quantum melibatkan langkah-langkah berikut:
examples.suggested: Menghasilkan Hamiltonian kimia kuantum untuk digunakan pada QPU IBM Quantum melibatkan langkah-langkah berikut:
examples.why: The opening 'Untuk' turns the sentence into a purpose clause with no main subject, so 'melibatkan' dangles. Grammatically awkward, though meaning is recoverable.

examples.source: since this will already be familiar to many of you
examples.translation: karena banyak dari kalian yang sudah familiar
examples.suggested: karena banyak dari kamu yang sudah familiar
examples.why: The file otherwise uses singular informal 'kamu/-mu' consistently; the lone plural 'kalian' here is a minor consistency slip in the second-person register.

editor_note: The prose is genuinely fluent and reads like a native Indonesian technical writer in the first half (Introduction through the "Two computational basis states" section) — the Fourier intuition, winding/phase explanations, and Heisenberg analogy are rendered naturally with consistent informal register ("kamu", "penggunaanmu", "akunmu"; no deferential bapak/ibu). Meaning is faithful throughout: I checked the load-bearing claims (even/odd basis peaks line 393/420, phase accumulation rate $2\pi y/N$ line 407, inverse width relationship line 281, 100%-certainty conditions for $\theta=0$ vs $\theta=1/2$ line 596) and found no semantic drift, inverted conditions, or swapped quantities. The issues are terminology-consistency slips, not mistranslations. The biggest is that "state" is rendered "keadaan" consistently through line 285 but then switches to the English loanword "state" from "Uraikan algoritma QFT" onward (line 425 "state qubit", 427 "state basis", and pervasively through 672) — the same concept appears two ways within one file. Secondarily, "circuit" is rendered as a capitalized English "Circuit" mid-sentence in an MT-artifact way ("Circuit kita" line 125, "Circuit QFT" line 516, "diagram Circuit-nya" line 577) rather than "sirkuit"/"rangkaian", and "eigenstate" (line 581) is left in English while sibling terms "nilai eigen"/"vektor eigen" are translated. None of these mislead a reader; core domain terms (qubit, gate, uniter, fase, Hadamard, basis komputasi/Fourier) are correct and the meaning teaches as well as the English.

examples.source: QFT$_2$ transforms computational basis states $|0\rangle$ and $|1\rangle$ ... what gates do we actually implement
examples.translation: QFT$_2$ mengubah state basis komputasi ... gate apa saja yang sebenarnya kita implementasikan
examples.suggested: QFT$_2$ mengubah keadaan basis komputasi ...
examples.why: "state" is translated as "keadaan" consistently through line 285 (e.g. "keadaan basis komputasi"), then switches to the untranslated loanword "state" from line 425 onward. The same concept rendered two ways within one file.

examples.source: selecting a device to run our circuit on
examples.translation: memilih perangkat untuk menjalankan Circuit kita
examples.suggested: memilih perangkat untuk menjalankan sirkuit kita
examples.why: Capitalized mid-sentence "Circuit" reads as an automated term-substitution artifact, not as something a native writer would produce; appears repeatedly ("Circuit QFT", "diagram Circuit-nya"). Either lowercase loanword or "sirkuit"/"rangkaian" would be natural.

examples.source: which is an eigenstate of $U$
examples.translation: yang merupakan eigenstate dari $U$
examples.suggested: yang merupakan keadaan eigen (eigenstate) dari $U$
examples.why: "eigenstate" is left in English while the closely related "nilai eigen" (eigenvalue) and "vektor eigen" (eigenvector) are translated in the same passages — a small internal inconsistency in the eigen-family terms.

editor_note: This is faithful, well-edited technical Italian that reads close to native and teaches as clearly as the English; meaning is preserved throughout with no semantic drift (negations, conditions, and all quantities — 1024 shot, 10 set, 28 randomizzazioni, (28, 10, 1, 3), "solo uno shot" — match the source exactly). Domain terminology is correct and consistent: the field-standard English terms are rightly kept (twirling, shot, gate, box, samplex, backend) and the conventional Italian neologisms are used uniformly ("transpilato"/"Transpiler", "registro classico", "set di parametri", "compito" for task, "classi contenitore"). Register is consistently informal-tu where the source addresses the reader ("Comprendi", "Ti consigliamo", "consulta", "aggiungi il tuo programma"); "consulta" is the tu imperative, not the formal Lei, so it is fine. The few slips are cosmetic and never mislead: (1) inconsistent capitalization of circuit — "Circuit di input" at L200/L206 vs "circuito di input" at L222, plus "Gli elementi Circuit" (L135) where lowercase "elementi samplex" is used nearby; (2) mild calque in "un compito diverso per Executor da eseguire" (L57), mirroring English "for Executor to perform" where a native writer would prefer "che Executor deve eseguire"; (3) the slightly awkward "una coppia Circuit template e samplex" (L135) for "a template circuit and a samplex pair". None of these rise to a terminology or accuracy defect.

examples.source: the classical registers' names in the input circuits
examples.translation: i nomi dei registri classici nei Circuit di input (L200) ... nel Circuit di input (L206) ... del circuito di input (L222)
examples.suggested: Use lowercase "circuito" consistently: "registri classici nei circuiti di input"
examples.why: "circuit" (the generic noun, not the class name) is rendered both as capitalized "Circuit" and as "circuito" within the same file; cosmetic inconsistency, not misleading.

examples.source: Each of these items represents a different task for Executor to perform.
examples.translation: Ognuno di questi elementi rappresenta un compito diverso per Executor da eseguire.
examples.suggested: Ognuno di questi elementi rappresenta un compito diverso che Executor deve eseguire.
examples.why: "per Executor da eseguire" calques the English "for Executor to perform"; a relative clause reads more naturally in Italian.

examples.source: It then generates a template circuit and a samplex pair using the build() function.
examples.translation: Genera poi una coppia Circuit template e samplex usando la funzione build().
examples.suggested: Genera poi un circuito template e un samplex usando la funzione build().
examples.why: "una coppia Circuit template e samplex" is stiff and re-introduces capitalized "Circuit"; the meaning (a template circuit plus a samplex) is preserved but the phrasing is slightly awkward.

editor_note: This is well-edited, fluent technical Italian that reads as if written by a native quantum-computing writer, not as machine translation. Domain terminology is conventional and fully consistent within the file: "fase globale"/"fase relativa", "misurazione nella base standard", "prodotto interno", "prodotto tensoriale", "operazione/vettore unitario", "matrice stocastica", "misurazione proiettiva", and the field-standard English retentions ("qubit", "gate", "no-cloning", "controlled-NOT", "workspace") are all handled correctly and uniformly; "discriminare/discriminati" is the right Italian QI rendering. The informal "tu" register holds throughout the probabilistic-state passage ("Immagina che qualcuno ti consegni", "tutto ciò che hai in mano", "non ti hanno detto"). Meaning is faithful across the no-cloning proof, the non-orthogonal discrimination proof, and every caveat (e.g. "precisione limitata", "clone approssimato"). The single defect is a subject-verb number disagreement on line 66: the plural subject "essi" (referring to the two states) is paired with the singular verb "differirà" where it must be "differiranno"; the meaning is still clear, so this is an isolated slip rather than semantic drift.

examples.source: ...they will always differ by a global phase...
examples.translation: ...essi differirà sempre per una fase globale...
examples.suggested: ...essi differiranno sempre per una fase globale...
examples.why: Subject-verb number disagreement: plural subject 'essi' (the two states) takes the singular verb 'differirà'. A native technical writer would write the plural 'differiranno'. Meaning is recoverable, so this is an isolated grammatical slip, not semantic drift.
